steps.title: Emphasize a bird difference
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize a difference between the great-billed hermit and the amethyst woodstar. The notes give contrastive traits for the two hummingbirds, including size and bill shape. The hermit is large and has a very long, curved bill, while the woodstar is small and has a short, straight bill.

steps.title: Choose the direct comparison
steps.content: Choice A describes only the great-billed hermit. Choice C says both birds are hummingbirds found in Suriname, which is a similarity. Choice D describes only the amethyst woodstar. The effective choice explicitly distinguishes the birds by comparing their bills, using curved for the hermit and straight for the woodstar. The answer is B.
